title: A-IC3: Learning-Guided Adaptive Inductive Generalization for Hardware Model Checking
authors: Xiaofeng Zhou, Guangyu Hu, Hongce Zhang, Wei Zhang
abstract: The IC3 algorithm represents the state-of-the-art (SOTA) hardware model checking technique, owing to its robust performance and scalability. A significant body of research has focused on enhancing the solving efficiency of the IC3 algorithm, with particular attention to the inductive generalization process: a critical phase wherein the algorithm seeks to generalize a counterexample to inductiveness (CTI), which typically is a state leading to a bad state, into a broader set of states. This inductive generalization is a primary source of clauses in IC3 and thus plays a pivotal role in determining the overall effectiveness of the algorithm. Despite its importance, existing approaches often rely on fixed inductive generalization strategies, overlooking the dynamic and context-sensitive nature of the verification environment in which spurious counterexamples arise. This rigidity can limit the quality of generated clauses and, consequently, the performance of IC3. To address this limitation, we propose a lightweight machine-learning-based framework that dynamically selects appropriate inductive generalization strategies in response to the evolving verification context. Specifically, we employ a multi-armed bandit (MAB) algorithm to adaptively choose inductive generalization strategies based on real-time feedback from the verification process. The agent is updated by evaluating the quality of generalization outcomes, thereby refining its strategy selection over time. Empirical evaluation on a benchmark suite comprising 914 instances, primarily drawn from the latest HWMCC collection, demonstrates the efficacy of our approach. When implemented on the state-of-the-art model checker rIC3, our method solves 26 to 50 more cases than the baselines and improves the PAR-2 score by 194.72 to 389.29.
title_zh: A-IC3：面向硬件模型检测的学习引导自适应归纳泛化
abstract_zh: IC3算法因其强大的性能和可扩展性，被认为是当前硬件模型检测领域的最先进技术（SOTA）。大量研究致力于提高IC3算法的求解效率，特别是关注其归纳泛化过程：这是一个关键阶段，算法试图将归纳反例（CTI，通常是导致错误状态的状态）泛化为更广泛的状态集合。归纳泛化是IC3中子句生成的主要来源，因此对算法整体效果起着决定性作用。尽管如此，现有方法通常依赖于固定的归纳泛化策略，忽视了验证环境中虚假反例出现的动态性和上下文敏感性。这种僵化性可能限制生成子句的质量，从而影响IC3的性能。为了解决这一局限性，我们提出了一种轻量级的基于机器学习的框架，该框架能够根据不断变化的验证上下文动态选择合适的归纳泛化策略。具体而言，我们采用多臂老虎机（MAB）算法，根据验证过程中的实时反馈自适应地选择归纳泛化策略。通过评估泛化结果的质量，代理不断更新并优化其策略选择。基于包含914个实例的基准测试套件（主要来自最新的HWMCC集合）的实证评估表明，我们的方法具有显著的效果。当在最先进的模型检测器rIC3上实现时，我们的方法比基线多解决26到50个实例，并将PAR-2分数提高了194.72到389.29。

title: LLM-Design Platform for Thermal-Failure-Aware 3D Chiplet Layout via Iterative Parameter Analysis
authors: Tai Song, Senling Wang, Xiaoqing Wen
abstract: Although advanced 3D chiplet packaging helps extend Moore’s-Law gains through vertical stacking of heterogeneous dies, it simultaneously introduces unprecedented thermal challenges. Shrinking 3D interconnects and rising current densities trap heat at through-silicon vias (TSVs) and micro-bumps, causing steep temperature gradients and thermo-mechanical stress that directly trigger early failures. Existing methods interact with finite-element analysis (FEA) tools mainly through manual, experience-based parameter tuning, inevitably making hot-spot detection time-consuming and error-prone. This paper establishes a text-interactive, thermal-failure-aware design framework for 3D chiplets that couples large language model (LLM)-accelerated thermal analysis with FEA simulation. A Python-driven Automatic Code Generation Model (ACGM) is invoked to automate geometry drawing, meshing, and boundary-condition assignment, enabling rapid analysis and prediction of hot-spot locations within densely stacked chiplets and TSVs. The proposed ACGM eliminates domain-expert dependence by abstracting cumbersome FEA parameter setting into natural-language commands, thereby lowering the entry barrier and cutting operation time. Deployment of the proposed ACGM streamlines intricate FEA operations and Python scripting into concise natural-language commands, enabling accurate layout-parameter tuning and shortened design cy-cles-establishing an AI-centric design-verification paradigm for advanced 3D chiplets.
title_zh: 用于热失效感知的3D芯粒布局迭代参数分析的LLM设计平台
abstract_zh: 尽管先进的3D芯粒封装通过异质芯片的垂直堆叠延续了摩尔定律的收益，但同时也带来了前所未有的热挑战。缩小的3D互连和不断上升的电流密度使热量困于硅通孔（TSV）和微凸点处，导致陡峭的温度梯度和热机械应力，从而直接引发早期失效。现有方法主要通过手动、基于经验的参数调整与有限元分析（FEA）工具交互，不可避免地使热点检测变得耗时且容易出错。本文建立了一个文本交互的热失效感知设计框架，用于3D芯粒，将大型语言模型（LLM）加速的热分析与FEA仿真相结合。一个基于Python的自动代码生成模型（ACGM）被调用，用于自动化几何绘制、网格划分和边界条件分配，从而快速分析和预测密集堆叠芯粒和TSV中的热点位置。所提出的ACGM通过将繁琐的FEA参数设置抽象为自然语言命令，消除了对领域专家的依赖，从而降低了使用门槛并缩短了操作时间。该ACGM的部署将复杂的FEA操作和Python脚本简化为简洁的自然语言命令，实现了精确的布局参数调整和缩短的设计周期——为先进的3D芯粒建立了一个以人工智能为中心的设计验证范式。
